page: Google Cloud Platform n4-highcpu-80 is a compute-optimized virtual machine belonging to the n4 server family. It features 80 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 40 physical Intel x86_64 cores with hyper-threading enabled. The system is equipped with 160.0 GB of system memory, yielding a memory-to-core ratio of 2.0 GB per core. It does not include local storage or integrated GPUs, and does not provide complimentary public IPv4 addresses. The processor features a 260 MB L3 cache, 2 MB L2 cache, and 48 KB L1d cache, alongside advanced instruction sets such as Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX_tile, AMX_int8) and Advanced Vector Extensions (AVX-512).

In benchmark evaluations, the instance demonstrates strong multi-core performance, placing in the top 25% for stress-ng multi-core CPU testing, Geekbench multi-core, and PassMark CPU scores. Its memory subsystem achieves strong read bandwidths of 5236.9 GB/sec for cached small blocks and 286.55 GB/sec for uncached large blocks. The server excels in static web serving throughput and Geekbench image processing, both ranking in the top 10% tier. Single-core performance, including Geekbench single-core and stress-ng single-core, falls within the average middle 50% range.

Given its high compute density and 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio, the n4-highcpu-80 offers qualitative cost efficiency for compute-heavy workloads that do not require massive memory footprints or local SSD storage. The inclusion of Intel AMX and AVX-512 instructions makes it highly capable for CPU-based machine learning tasks, as evidenced by strong prompt processing speeds on small and medium LLM models. It is highly suited for multi-threaded compilation, database operations, high-throughput static web serving, and parallelized data decompression tasks.
description: Google Cloud Platform n4-highcpu-80 is an x86_64 compute-optimized server featuring 80 dedicated vCPUs across 40 physical Intel cores and 160.0 GB of RAM. Lacking local storage and GPUs, this instance relies on its high-density processing capabilities, supported by a 260 MB L3 cache and advanced instruction sets like Intel AMX and AVX-512. Benchmark results show top 10% performance in static web serving throughput and image processing, alongside strong top 25% multi-core CPU and memory bandwidth metrics. Conversely, single-core performance remains in the average tier. This hardware profile provides a cost-efficient solution for compute-bound workloads that require high parallel processing power without massive memory overhead. It is highly suited for software compilation, database operations, multi-threaded decompression, and CPU-based machine learning inference.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform n4-highcpu-80 is an x86_64 compute-optimized server with 80 dedicated Intel vCPUs, 160 GB RAM, and advanced AMX instruction sets, delivering strong multi-core and web serving performance.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform n4-highcpu-80 features 80 dedicated Intel vCPUs, 160 GB RAM, and strong multi-core performance for compute-heavy workloads.
tagline: A high-density, compute-optimized x86_64 platform featuring eighty dedicated vCPUs and advanced matrix extensions for parallelized workloads.
bullet_points: 80 dedicated Intel vCPUs and 160 GB of RAM (2.0 GB per core)
Intel AMX and AVX-512 extensions for accelerated matrix and vector operations
Top 10% performance in static web serving and image processing benchmarks
Strong multi-core processing and high-bandwidth memory architecture
Best suited for software compilation, database operations, and CPU-based LLM inference
